text: . . Reading a number before a String - Watch out! When reading a String after an int something unexpected happens — the user doesn’t get a chance to enter their name, the program seems to skip this input and displays the output with an empty string for their name. System.out.print( "How old are you: " ); The output looks like this: age = input.nextInt(); How old are you: 17 System.out.print( "What is your full name: " ); What is your full name: Well, , you are 17 years old name = input.nextLine(); System.out.print( "Well, " + name); System.out.println( ", you are " + age + " years old." ); The reason this happens is that the nextInt() method only reads digits (for an int) from the stream, it does not remove the newline (\n) that the user typed after their age 1 7 m L 0o o W After call from nextInt(), this is where nextLine() starts reading from The arrow indicates the next character to be read by the Scanner. When we call nextLine() to read the name, it reads the \n as an empty string “”, as if the user simply pressed <Return>when asked for their name! Any other input is left behind on the input stream.
